item.title: "The Abstract Show" will be displayed from April 1 -25. Intake will be from March 25-28. - 04/16/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/30/2026 8:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Get ready for a bold, vibrant, and unpredictable art experience! The Abstract Art Exhibit is all about letting go, embracing creativity, and seeing the world through an entirely new lens. Whether you're an artist or an admirer, this is a show that will challenge, inspire, and excite both the creator and the viewer! Abstract art is more than just shapes and colors, it's about emotion, movement, and limitless expression. Every piece in this exhibit pushes the boundaries of imagination, drawing you into a world where rules are meant to be broken, and where creativity knows no limits. Expect a dazzling mix of mediums, including photography, painting, sculpture, textiles, digital art, jewelry, pastels, glass, and more. If you can dream it, it belongs here! Whether youÕre showcasing your work or simply soaking in the creativity, donÕt miss this one-of-a kind exhibit. Step inside, explore, and let your imagination run wild!
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: 13th Annual Protective Services 5K - 04/18/2026 8:30am EDT to 04/18/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description:
Location : 491 Coe St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Join Us for the 13th Annual Protective Services 5K!

Get ready to lace up your sneakers and hit the pavement with fellow enthusiasts at this fun, in-person event to promote child abuse awareness and prevention. Whether you're a seasoned runner or prefer to walk, the 13th Annual Protective Services 5K is perfect for everyone. Bring your dog too! Come enjoy the energy, the community spirit, and show your support! Don’t miss out on the action—see you at the starting line! We will start at the American Legion Shelter at Hedges Boyer Park and follow the Rock Creek Trail.

We've experienced some sprinkles in years past and we don't let that stop us. In the event of significant rain, the event will be held at Frann's Fieldhouse, on the Heidelberg campus. Please watch our Facebook page (Seneca County JFS) for updates.

Dogs are welcome. Please have them on a leash and be sure to pick up anything they leave behind.

This is a free event.

8:30 am: Registration and packet pick-up

8:50 am: Opening Remarks

9: 00 am: Runners Start

9:05 am: Walkers Start
item.dc:creator: Seneca County JFS

item.title: Tiffin Moose Lodge Monthly All You Care to Eat Breakfast Buffet - 04/18/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/18/2026 11:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 1146 OH-53, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin Moose Lodge Monthly All You Care to Eat Breakfast Buffet April 18th 9 a.m. - 11:00 a.m. Open to the public. The cost is $10 for ages 11 and up. $5 ages 6-10. Kids 5 and under eat free. Menu: Scrambled Eggs Hashbrowns Sausage gravy and biscuits French Toast Sticks, Sausage patties Fruit Cocktail Coffee. The social quarters will be open so that you may purchase other beverage options including Bloody Mary's, Clamdiggers and more. Please help spread the word and share this event.
item.dc:creator: The Tiffin Moose - Lodge 846

item.title: Breakfast at JAM’s Markets SATURDAYS 9-2pm - 04/18/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/18/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 144 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Enjoy from scratch made breakfast and lunch items featuring buzzworthy sourdough artisan breads
item.dc:creator: JAM's Monthly Markets

item.title: Speed Dating Event at the Tiffin Community Kitchen - 04/18/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/18/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 89 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Hey there! Ready to dive into a new relationship? Join us for a fun Speed Dating Event on April 18th! You'll enjoy great conversations, tasty appetizers, and refreshing drinks while meeting new people. We can't wait to see you there! Click the link to sign up today! https://tiffin-seneca-farmers-market.square.site/
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Community Kitchen

item.title: Into the Woods - 04/18/2026 7:00pm EDT to 04/18/2026 9:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : University Hall, 310 E Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre presents Into the Woods.

Be careful what you wish for... 🌲✨

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre invites you to a spellbinding production of Stephen Sondheim and James Lapine’s Into the Woods. Watch as the worlds of Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, and Jack and the Beanstalk collide in a darkly comic exploration of what happens after "happily ever after."

Join a baker and his wife on their quest to break a witch's curse—and discover the true cost of getting everything you ever wanted.

📍 Where: Gundlach Theatre 

📅 When: * April 16, 17, & 18 | 7:00 PM

April 19 | 2:00 PM

Tickets:

 https://www.eventbrite.com/cc/into-the-woods-4810285

Poster Design by Bri Clark, BA’23 and MBA’24

Into the Woods is presented through special arrangement with Music Theatre International (MTI).

 All authorized performance materials are also supplied by MTI.

www.mtishows.com
item.dc:creator: Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre

item.title: Rick Springfield - 04/18/2026 7:30pm EDT to 04/18/2026 8:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 30 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Saturday, April 18, 2026 – 7:30 PM Over the past [...]
item.dc:creator: The Ritz Theatre

item.title: Rick Springfield - 04/18/2026 7:30pm EDT to 04/18/2026 9:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 30 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Over the past four decades, Rick Springfield has worn many hats as an entertainer and performer. The creator of some of the finest power-pop of the ’80s, a Grammy winning singer, songwriter, and musician who has sold 25 million albums and scored 17 U.S. Top 40 hits, including “Jessie’s Girl,” “Don’t Talk to Strangers,” “An Affair of the Heart,” “I’ve Done Everything for You,” “Love Somebody,” and “Human Touch.” He’s an accomplished actor who has starred opposite Meryl Streep in the feature film “Ricki and the Flash,” gave a chameleonic performance as the creepy Dr. Irving Pitlor in HBO’s prestige drama “True Detective,” earned great reviews for his portrayal of Lucifer on the CW hit “Supernatural” and most recently played the insane Pastor Charles on FX’s worldwide hit series “American Horror Story.” An author as well, both his candid 2010 memoir Late, Late at Night (which Rolling Stone named one of the 25 greatest rock memoirs of all time) and his 2014 comedic novel Magnificent Vibration earned rave reviews and spots on the New York Times Best Sellers’ list. In 2014, Springfield was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, located around the corner from the first apartment he lived in when he first arrived in the U.S. from Australia in 1971. Springfield’s Sirius-XM radio show, Working Class DJ, is a huge hit and airs weekly on 80s on 8. His new album “Automatic” is out now. Automatic is his first album of all new original material in 5 years.

$125, $100, $75

Fan Club tickets go on sale November 10 at 10:00 AM

Public ticket sales begin November 17 at 10:00 AM.
item.dc:creator: The Ritz Theatre

item.title: Into the Woods - 04/19/2026 2:00pm EDT to 04/19/2026 4:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : University Hall, 310 E Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre presents Into the Woods.

Be careful what you wish for... 🌲✨

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre invites you to a spellbinding production of Stephen Sondheim and James Lapine’s Into the Woods. Watch as the worlds of Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, and Jack and the Beanstalk collide in a darkly comic exploration of what happens after "happily ever after."

Join a baker and his wife on their quest to break a witch's curse—and discover the true cost of getting everything you ever wanted.

📍 Where: Gundlach Theatre 

📅 When: * April 16, 17, & 18 | 7:00 PM

April 19 | 2:00 PM

Tickets:

 https://www.eventbrite.com/cc/into-the-woods-4810285

Poster Design by Bri Clark, BA’23 and MBA’24

Into the Woods is presented through special arrangement with Music Theatre International (MTI).

 All authorized performance materials are also supplied by MTI.

www.mtishows.com

item.title: Dine to Donate - 04/20/2026 3:00pm EDT to 04/20/2026 10:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Dine for a Cause! Join us on April 20, 2026 for a delicious way to give back! Head over to Xcaret Mexican Resturaunt and enjoy your favorite meals while supporting the Tiffin-Seneca Heritage Festival 15% of all sales from the day will go directly to the festival—helping continue 46 years of celebrating history, community, and unforgettable memories! Date: April 20, 2026 Location: Xcaret Mexican Food Bring your friends, bring your appetite, and help make a difference—one taco at a time
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Seneca Heritage Festival

item.title: 3p | Teen Tuesday - 04/21/2026 3:00pm EDT to 04/21/2026 4:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA null
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: CASA Volunteer Training Orientation - 04/21/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/21/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 21 Court St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Imagine being a child, moving from home to home… Never knowing who to trust. Never having someone to speak up for you. Now imagine YOU being the person who changes that story. A steady voice. A safe place. A lifeline in a difficult time. Our Spring Volunteer Training Orientation is Tuesday, April 21 at 5:00 PM at our Tiffin office (21 Court Street). For about 8 hours a month, you can help a child look back on their childhood with gratitude instead of regret. No legal background required — just heart, time, and a commitment to stand by a child when they need it most. Learn more or apply at www.casaofssw.org or call 419-448-1442. Because right now, there are children who desperately need you.
item.dc:creator: CASA of SSW

item.title: "Colored Pencil Blueberries and Spoon" Wednesday, April 22 , 9am-12pm #04222601 - 04/22/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/22/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA In this class you will use colored pencils to create a 5"x7" drawing of a blueberry spoon, learning about strokes and blending techniques. All supplies are provided. Preregistration is required by Friday March 6th, minimum class size is 3 people. The cost is $45 for TAG members & $55 for non-members. Contact Mike Fink (813-695-0506), or TAG (419-443-0478), or tiffinartguild@att.net with questions. You can also stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: CHIPOTLE FUNDRAISER FOR ALLEN EIRY CENTER - 04/22/2026 4:00pm EDT to 04/22/2026 5:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 28 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for a Chipotle fundraiser supporting the Allen Eiry Center, happening from 4 PM to 8 PM. Enjoy delicious food while contributing to a great cause!
item.dc:creator: Allen Eiry Center

item.title: Shred Day at Your Legacy FCU - 04/23/2026 10:00am EDT to 04/23/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 25 Shaffer Park Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Get your stuff together and ready to come drop off your confidential and outdated personal mail and documents! Last year, we collected 1933 lbs. of material, an average to 56.85 LBS. per person. and we'd like to blow it out of the water! Make plans to drop it off on Thursday, April 23rd from 10-2 in our parking lot and we'll be happy to unload your vehicle and take it from there. See you then!
item.dc:creator: Your Legacy Federal Credit Union

item.title: 5p | Print It! Build It! Move It! - 04/23/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/23/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA null
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: "Colored Pencil Apple" Thursday, April 23, 6pm-9pm #04232602 - 04/23/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/23/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA In this class you will use colored pencils to create a 5"x7" drawing of an apple, learning about strokes and blending techniques. All supplies are provided. Preregistration is required by Saturday, April 18. Minimum class size is 3 people. The cost is $45 for TAG members & $55 for non-members. Contact Mike Fink (813-695-0506), or TAG (419-443-0478), or tiffinartguild@att.net with questions. You can also stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: LIFEGUARDING COURSE - 04/24/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/26/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 180 Summit St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Ready to make a difference this summer? 🌊☀️

Become a certified lifeguard and gain the skills to save lives, build confidence, and become a leader!

Important Details:

APRIL 24-26, 2026

Friday 4p-8p, Saturday 8a-5p, & Sunday 8a-5p

• Blended course format – 7 hours of online coursework must be completed before the first day of class

• Swim assessment required one week prior to the first class- Friday at 6pm

• Attendance is mandatory for all scheduled days (no make-up sessions)

• Registration closes one week before the class begins

• Limited to just 8 students per class – reserve your spot early!

Don’t wait—spots fill fast! 📅 Enroll now at the Tiffin Community YMCA and dive into a new opportunity!
item.dc:creator: YMCA of Bucyrus-Tiffin, Inc

item.title: 9:30a | Friends of the Library Book Sale - 04/24/2026 9:30am EDT to 04/24/2026 5:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA null
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Volunteer Fair - 04/24/2026 10:00am EDT to 04/24/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 28 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Come join us for the Volunteer Fair on April 24th! Discover meaningful ways to make a difference in your community and connect with a variety of organizations looking for passionate volunteers. Free giveaways and raffles! Interested in hosting a table? Please contact Jaime Cole RSVP Coordinator at 859-551-9605 or jaime.cole@axessfamily.org
item.dc:creator: Retired & Senior Volunteer Program

item.title: Downtown Tiffin Clean Up ~ April 2026 - 04/25/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/25/2026 10:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for a Downtown Tiffin Clean-Up on Saturday, April 25, 2026, from 9:00–10:30 AM! We’ll meet at Cherry Alley, and we’ll have everything you need (trash bags, brooms, dust-pans, and gloves). Who can volunteer? Everyone! Come on your own, bring a friend, or build a group and join "Bunky’s Litter League", a community-wide initiative focused on litter cleanup and beautification. Teams can include: Businesses Neighborhood groups Faith-based organizations Civic and service groups Clubs Families, individuals, and more... Volunteers are what make this event possible. Your help keeps downtown clean, welcoming, and vibrant. Please register to volunteer by clicking the “Ticket” tab. Save the dates (more clean-ups!) June 27, 2026 September 12, 2026 November 21, 2026 (Leaf Clean-Up)
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Breakfast at JAM’s Markets SATURDAYS 9-2pm - 04/25/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/25/2026 2:00pm EDT

item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA null
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Earth Day 5k Event - 04/25/2026 10:00am EDT to 04/25/2026 11:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 194 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Registration ~ 9:00am 5k Race ~ 10:00am Post Race Refreshments Register Required at https://forms.gle/ctvzEmXvTggzUPeX9 NO ENTRY FEE Details: -Dress like a Chicken -Prizes awarded to Top 3 finishers -Water stop at halfway point -Paved Path for walkers & strollers -Course includes trail in the woods Recycled Content T-Shirts Available for Purchase for $20 (Sizes: Adult S, M, L, XL, and 2XL + Kids S, M, L, and XL) Please Park in ESA Marked Parking Lot
item.dc:creator: Franciscan Earth Literacy Center

item.title: Day Of Giving For The Famous Aunt Ellen! - 04/26/2026 8:00am EDT to 04/26/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA If you know of Big Mike's, then you most definitely know The Famous Aunt Ellen. Words one could use to describe her are strong, faithful, funny, caring, kind, and selfless. Always been one to help someone else where I think accepting help is a hard thing for her to take. I am grateful that she has said yes to allowing us to put this event on for her to show her just how much we love her and want to help her in this battle! For anyone who does not know, Aunt Ellen was diagnosed with pancreatic cancer in the fall. She is currently doing chemotherapy and is responding really well to it thus far. We are hoping to raise money to help Aunt Ellen with medical bills during this difficult time. If you wish to donate, please reach out to me so I can add you on our list. We will be selling $10 raffle tickets for items to win with all the proceeds going right to The Famous Aunt Ellen! I know items already donated are a 2 hour ride party bike ride and 1 night stay at The Melmore Cabin! Many more cool prizes will be announced as they come in! We will be serving food all day long. We hope to have entertainment for kids and a band on the roof again! We will announce these options as we get them confirmed!
item.dc:creator: Big Mike's

item.title: "I Choose Myself Today" A Peaceful Sunday Devoted to You - 04/26/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/26/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 2044 S State Rte 53, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA null
item.dc:creator: SOUL Boutique

item.title: Annual Community Earth Day Event - 04/26/2026 1:00pm EDT to 04/26/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 194 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Lots of raffle drawings! Bring your bags of aluminum cans to recycle! Lots of hands on Activity Stations! FREE fun for the entire family! Bagged Aluminum Cans can be dropped off at the Seeds of Hope Farm Pavilion between Friday, April 24 - Sunday, April 26. Participating Organizations Include: -Franciscan Earth Literacy Center -Tiffin Parks and Recreation, -Seneca County Park District -Tiffin Water Pollution Control Center -Seneca Conservation District -Glass Roots -OSS Solid Waste District -Columbian High School Bio Club -Sisters of Francis Mission Core Team -National Center for Water Quality Research at Heidelberg University -OSU Ext Master Gardeners Volunteer Program of Seneca County
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Park & Recreation

item.title: Mobile Mammography - 04/27/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/27/2026 1:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 1344 W Seneca Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Mercy Health Mobile Mammography Van provides services for easier access to quality care in a safe, convenient environment. The mobile unit uses the same newest high-quality 3D technology, including patient assisted compression, allowing you to control the amount of pressure once in position for your mammogram, allowing for a more comfortable experience.
item.dc:creator: Mercy Health - Springfield

item.title: Let's Talk About It - 04/27/2026 10:30am EDT to 04/27/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Tiffin Seneca Public Library for the adult book discussion group, “Let’s Talk About It,” on Monday, April 27 at 10:30 a.m. in the Junior Home Room. The group will explore the 2026 Community Read selection, The Astral Library by Kate Quinn. Copies of the book are available at the Library, and everyone is welcome to attend this free event without registration. Refreshments will be provided. For more details, please call the Library at 419-447-3751 or visit their online calendar at www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: The Fabulous Fifties - 04/28/2026 10:30am EDT to 04/28/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 28 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for "The Fabulous Fifties," an exciting event open to the public! Pre-registration is required, so please call 419-448-9774 or stop by for more information. Don't miss out on this nostalgic celebration!
item.dc:creator: Allen Eiry Center

item.title: Ribbon Cutting | Tiffin Rotary Club 100th Anniversary - 04/28/2026 11:30am EDT to 04/28/2026 12:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 72 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca Regional Chamber & Development Ambassadors in celebrating a historic milestone with the Tiffin Rotary Club, as they mark their 100th Anniversary with a ribbon cutting and clock dedication ceremony on Tuesday, April 28th at 11:30 AM. As part of this celebration, the Tiffin Rotary Club will officially gift the Rotary clock, located in City Lot 7, to the City of Tiffin, commemorating a century of service, leadership, and community impact. Since its founding, Rotary has been dedicated to fostering friendship, developing leaders, and creating meaningful change both locally and around the world. The Tiffin Rotary Club exemplifies these values through its commitment to service and its ongoing efforts to strengthen the community. Celebrate 100 years of Rotary in Tiffin, honor this lasting contribution to our downtown, and recognize the individuals who continue to make a difference through service above self.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: 3p | Teen Tuesday - 04/28/2026 3:00pm EDT to 04/28/2026 4:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA null
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Kate Quinn Author Visit - 04/28/2026 6:30pm EDT to 04/28/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin-Seneca Public Library is excited to announce "The Astral Library" by Kate Quinn as the 2026 Christopher D. English Community Read title. Each year the Community Read encourages people in Seneca County to read the selected title and come together for special events and discussions. As the featured event in the Community Read, Kate Quinn, a New York Times best-selling author, will speak to the community on Tuesday, April 28 at 6:30 p.m. at the Chandelier Community Event Center. The author event is completely free and open to the community and will be followed by a book signing. Copies of The Astral Library will be available for purchase thanks to the Pan Yan Bookstore both at the author event and at the Bookstore leading up to it. "The Astral Library" is “a gorgeously written fantastical adventure which poses the question: Have you ever wished you could live inside a book? Welcome to the Astral Library, where books are not just objects, but doors to new worlds, new lives, and new futures.” Just released in February, "The Astral Library" became an instant bestseller by reaching #2 in the New York Times Bestsellers list and #1 on the Globe & Mail bestsellers list in it’s first week of publication. Copies of the book will be available soon to be checked out from the Library, and the eBook and audiobook can be found on the Ohio Digital Library and Libby app. Additional events accompanying the author visit, including an adult craft night, book discussion, and more will be released in greater detail at a later date. The Christopher D. English Community Read was recently named in memory of former library trustee Christopher D. English and encourages Seneca County residents to read, think about, and discuss one outstanding title each spring, culminating in the author giving a public presentation in Tiffin. Past speakers have included New York Times best-selling authors Shelby Van Pelt, Kristin Harmel, Sarah Penner and Michael Hingson, Pulitzer Prize finalist and LA Times columnist Steve Lopez, and international best-selling author Janet Beard. The series is made possible through generous funding by the Tiffin-Seneca Library Foundation and the many donors who have contributed to the Foundation over the years. For more information call 419-447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/community-read.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Seneca Proud Game Night Party - 04/28/2026 6:30pm EDT to 04/28/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 125 E Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA This month's event is a family friendly community game night featuring board and card games for all ages. We will meet in the undercroft of Old Trinity and provide a variety of board and card games to play along with instruction as needed and light refreshments. You are welcome to bring a treat and/or game to share as you are able. Join us for a night of fun and community!
item.dc:creator: Seneca Proud

item.title: Mobile Mammography - 04/30/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/30/2026 1:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 1344 W Seneca Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Mercy Health Mobile Mammography Van provides services for easier access to quality care in a safe, convenient environment. The mobile unit uses the same newest high-quality 3D technology, including patient assisted compression, allowing you to control the amount of pressure once in position for your mammogram, allowing for a more comfortable experience.
item.dc:creator: Mercy Health - Springfield

item.title: 2026 Seneca CEO Trade Show - 04/30/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/30/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Seneca County, OH, USA Save the date! More details to come.
item.dc:creator: Seneca CEO

item.title: Celebrate 30 Years—Nature Craft - 04/30/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/30/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Fort Seneca, OH 44883, USA In observance of the Seneca County Park District’s 30th Anniversary, join park staff for a nature craft. Have an interesting nature item, feel free to bring it along. All nature items and supplies will be provided. Just bring your creativity for a fun time. Registration is required at least 24 hours in advance of the program.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Parks

item.title: April Book Club - 04/30/2026 6:30pm EDT to 04/30/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 137 E Perry St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Our book club is open to the public. Everyone is welcome. Reading the book is recommended but discussion is open for those who haven't finished reading yet! The book for April is Giovanni's Room by James Baldwin. Books are available from the library and for purchase at Pan Yan Bookstore in downtown Tiffin.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Proud

item.title: Financial Fitness Friday - 05/01/2026 8:30am EDT to 05/01/2026 5:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 25 Shaffer Park Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join our Financial Counselor Rusti Claydon for Financial Fitness Friday, a new monthly event for our membership for FREE walk-in assistance and education surrounding your financial concerns! The first Friday of each month, Rusti has cleared her schedule to allow for members of the credit union to stop in and ask questions about their situation, ask for insights into how we can assist and how to better utilize Your Legacy Federal Credit Union offerings to their benefit! Not a member? Rusti can get you started with just a $5 minimum initial share. See you there!
item.dc:creator: Your Legacy Federal Credit Union

item.title: Annual Plant Sale at the Seeds of Hope Farm - 05/02/2026 8:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 194 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA You'll find a variety of chemical - free flowers, as well as cabbage, cauliflower, broccoli, tomatoes, eggplant, peppers, herbs and more! With a large variety of flower and veggies at just $2.00/4-pack, we have everything you need to get your gardens off to a great start. There will be microgreens and hydroponic lettuce and greenhouse tomatoes, and fresh eggs for sale, too.
item.dc:creator: Franciscan Earth Literacy Center

item.title: "Colored Pencil Rose" Saturday, May 2, 2026 9am-12pm #05022601 - 05/02/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA In this class you will use colored pencils to create a 5"x7" drawing of a rose, learning about strokes and blending techniques. All supplies are provided. Preregistration is required by Wednesday, April 29. Minimum class size is 3 people. The cost is $45 for TAG members & $55 for non-members. Contact Mike Fink (813-695-0506), or TAG (419-443-0478), or tiffinartguild@att.net with questions. You can also stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: Breakfast at JAM’s Markets SATURDAYS 9-2pm - 05/02/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 144 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Enjoy from scratch made breakfast and lunch items featuring buzzworthy sourdough artisan breads
item.dc:creator: JAM's Monthly Markets

item.title: Wildflower Hike - 05/02/2026 12:00pm EDT to 05/02/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 194 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join Naturalist Leonard Weber for a spring walk through the beautiful St. Francis woods viewing wildflowers while also paying attention to birds and other spring highlights. Spring is a wonderful time to explore our woods. Wear comfortable shoes for walking through the trails. Participants are welcome to drive to the woods. RSVP’s are encouraged, but not required at 419-448-7485. Donations are welcome.
item.dc:creator: Franciscan Earth Literacy Center

item.title: Out of Sight…Out of Murder - 05/02/2026 7:30pm EDT to 05/02/2026 8:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 30 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA A Mystery Comedy by Fred Carmichael A Ritz Players Production [...]
item.dc:creator: The Ritz Theatre
